title: Switzerland XI vs Qatar - Predicted lineup & team news for FIFA World Cup 2026 clash
original_text: Switzerland eye a big win over Asian Champions in their opening game of the World Cup in Santa Clara. Framed by the rugged Santa Cruz Mountains, the San Francisco Bay Area Stadium in Santa Clara gears up to host the opening matchweek fixture of Group B in the FIFA World Cup 2026. Switzerland take on reigning Asian champions Qatar. Rossocrociati went on to advance past the group stages in the last edition alongside heavyweights like Brazil, Cameroon and Serbia. This was before Portugal thrashed them 6-1 in the knockout rounds. They are led by the veteran Murat Yakin, who has been at the helm of Swiss football since 2021. Under Yakin, Switzerland reached the quarter-finals of Euro 2024. They are also one of the favourites to top Group B. These two sides last met in a friendly game in November 2018. A solitary late goal from Akram Afif sealed a thrilling win for the Asian outfit. After a 4-1 win over Jordan, Switzerland drew the last game against Australia. Talking about Qatar, they are shockingly winless in their last five games. In this article, we take a closer look at the predicted lineup of Switzerland for the big game against Qatar. Also read: Australia XI vs Turkey – Predicted lineup & team news for FIFA World Cup 2026 clash Switzerland team news vs Qatar Gregor Kobel of Dortmund in action during a Bundesliga match (Courtesy: DFL/Bundesliga) Murat Yakin will have a tough time selecting his best eleven out of a talented pool of Swiss players, who are in terrific form. Despite Breel Embolo coming after a fantastic season, there is a high chance that Burnley forward Zeki Amdouni will start. The defence looks solid with Dortmund keeper Kobel between the sticks. Who better than Inter defender Akanji marshalling the defence? They will have the experience of Xhaka and Ndoye in the midfield. Their major weakness is that they lack an elite, clinical number nine. Indeed, Embolo is brilliant in hold-up play, but the strikers often find it difficult to convert opportunities into goals. Switzerland XI vs Qatar Switzerland predicted lineup (4-2-3-1): Gregor Kobel (GK); Silvan Widmer, Nico Elvedi, Manuel Akanji, Ricardo Rodriguez; Remo Freuler, Granit Xhaka; Dan Ndoye, Fabian Rieder, Ruben Vargas; Zeki Amdouni. What happened when Switzerland last played in the FIFA World Cup? Rossocrociati went on to advance past the group stages in the last edition alongside heavyweights like Brazil, Cameroon and Serbia before Portugal thrashed them 6-1 in the knockout rounds. Which group are Switzerland in the FIFA World Cup 2026? Group B. Who else is in Switzerland’s group in the FIFA World Cup 2026?
